vibe: Deep electronic explorations — from French disco-house to Japanese ambient, UK post-dubstep to downtempo grooves. A journey through the margins of Rusty's sound.

tracks.name: Baby I'm Yours
tracks.reason: French disco-house adjacent to Folamour's boogie side — infectious synth funk

tracks.name: Kemuri
tracks.album: The Man From Mo' Wax (Original Motion Picture Soundtrack)
tracks.reason: Japanese hip-hop pioneer adjacent to Nujabes — smoky, cinematic boom-bap

tracks.name: Blue Train Lines
tracks.reason: Post-dubstep energy bridging Four Tet and Burial territory with King Krule vocals

tracks.name: Butterflies
tracks.reason: Lush UK deep house adjacent to Chris Stussy and Folamour — warm, melodic rollers

tracks.name: Les Nuits
tracks.reason: Downtempo classic that bridges Massive Attack's trip-hop with deep house warmth

tracks.name: Azukiiro No Kaori
tracks.reason: Japanese ambient master adjacent to Ryuichi Sakamoto — fragile beauty in sound

tracks.name: Ancestors
tracks.reason: Tokyo-based producer blending world percussion with electronic production — Nujabes-adjacent

tracks.name: Say That
tracks.reason: Chillwave-meets-house adjacent to Tame Impala and Khruangbin vibes

tracks.name: Paper Planes
tracks.reason: Glitchy Japanese electronic producer adjacent to Cornelius and Aphex Twin

tracks.name: Another Lifetime
tracks.reason: Futuristic R&B with Sade's smoothness and electronic sheen — silky vocals
